prompts.label: Daily recording
prompts.message: Help me write a daily record — calm breakfast, chose toast, watched TV before handover.
prompts.instant_prelude_text: I'm treating this as a daily recording question. Start with what happened, what the young person said or showed, how staff responded, and what happened next.
prompts.answer_preview:
I'm treating this as a daily recording question Start with what happened, what the young person said or showed, how The adult responded, and what happened next

prompts.label: Missing from care
prompts.message: A young person is missing from care right now. What should staff do?
prompts.instant_prelude_text: This looks like a missing-from-care concern. Follow the home's missing procedure, inform the manager/on-call, and record times, actions and decisions clearly.
prompts.answer_preview:
Missing return — immediate actions on shift:

Immediate welfare check

Calm welcome back — check injuries, distress, intoxication, hunger, fatigue and immediate medical need
Do not accuse or shame

Missing procedure

Follow local missing procedure
Update police if the missing episode was still active
Notify manager/on-call
Notify social worker/placing authority; EDT if out of hours

Health and safety

Consider health advice — 111 or 999 if unwell or intoxicated

Contextual safeguarding

Remain c

prompts.label: Allegation / LADO
prompts.message: A young person alleged a member of staff grabbed them.
prompts.instant_prelude_text: This looks like an allegation involving staff. Make sure the young person is safe, preserve their exact words, inform the manager/on-call, and follow the LADO/local allegations route.
prompts.answer_preview:
IndiCare is a residential children's homes operating system and intelligence platform built to support staff and managers in registered homes.

It is designed around care recording, safeguarding, Ofsted and SCCIF readiness, Quality Standards, governance, workforce support and reflective practice. The aim is to simplify recording and oversight while making records more child-centred, evidence-led and easier to review.

Key areas include Care Hub (command centre), Record, Young People, Chronology,

prompts.label: Self-harm
prompts.message: Young person disclosed self-harm and said they want to die.
prompts.instant_prelude_text: This may involve immediate safety. Stay with the young person if there is concern, inform the manager/on-call, and follow the home's safeguarding and self-harm procedure.

prompts.label: Medication refusal
prompts.message: A young person refused medication. What should we consider?

prompts.label: ORB Communicate
prompts.message: Create a communication support pack for a hospital visit tomorrow.
prompts.instant_prelude_text: I'm treating this as a communication support request. I'll create clear language, visual card suggestions, staff guidance and reflect-and-record prompts.
prompts.answer_preview:
Easy-read explanation

What is happening This easy-read is about the hospital visit or appointment. The young person needs to understand: Create a communication support pack for a hospital visit tomorrow..

Why it is happening Adults want everyone to understand what is happening. This matters because it affects the young person and the people who support them.

What happens next A trusted adult will explain step by step. There will be time to ask questions, use communication support or take a br

prompts.label: Child voice / AAC
prompts.message: How can I evidence a young person's voice when they communicate mainly through gestures and symbols?
prompts.instant_prelude_text: I'm treating this as AAC / symbols / gestures child voice evidence. Record what was observed, how the young person communicated, and do not invent direct quotes.
